reviews.review_description:
​Regional Connectivity (The Solution)

​I'm guessing that all the maps in Cities: VR are currently the same size because of the processing power required by the Quest hardware. If that is not the case, then there is no reason not to have much larger maps available for players. My suggestion to fix this, which I hope does not fall on deaf ears or blind eyes, is to allow users to connect multiple smaller cities together so they can trade and do business with each other. This would solve the issue of small map sizes without overloading the headset.

​Instead of trying to run one massive, continuous simulation that eats up all the processing power, the game would simply freeze City (A) while the user is working on City (B). These cities would only communicate through basic data variables, such as City (A) exporting a certain amount of electricity while importing a specific number of workers from City (B). This method would effectively allow for an unlimited number of connected maps, with the only real limitation being the user's storage space. A great way to implement this would be to allow a general region or state to consist of a 3x3 grid, which would allow players manage up to nine different cities or towns within a single interconnected state. Also allowing public transportation (roads, trains/Subways, and buses, through neighboring grids.

And if you decide to run with this idea, I would then sometime after; add natural disasters, and allow neighboring cities/towns to assist.
 
I would appreciate some feedback one this.

reviews.review_title: Fun game… but crashes very often.
reviews.review_description:
This is truly a fun game. I’ve spent hours and hours playing this game. 
However, as your city grows bigger, the game crashes all the time. I noticed that it is especially susceptible to crashing when Im working on my metro or bus lines. It’s getting to the point where it crashes every 30 secs or so. It’s is getting VERY frustrating.

I have city skylines on my xbox and now I have it on my VR and my PC what else you gonna be on a Nokia and anyways I love this game ￼￼

No expansion ability how am I supposed to make the best city if I can’t expand 

Not worth $20 ￼￼ should be $10 ￼￼

My city is plum full and I can't expanded to other
sections?  Am I missing something or is that it.  Will change to 5 star since it's fun but becomes too crowded too quickly.with expansion addition/explanation
You can go street level and see the little people as they go about their day. Impressive for VR.
Needs UPDATES
Took me 3 different times to find the best way to build the city. Now I am loving this.

It’s a good game but the pipes are taking me forever and it is not working I’ve placed 100 pipes!
 

All so it is very hard and not worth it

I’m coming in with minimal experience to the City Skylines games. I always played the roller coaster tycoon games and Jurassic World. For me this game seems like a great 1st attempt at vr adaptation. The controls are intuitive and the world feels busy. Maybe the game was in a bad place on launch but this felt really fun to me. I will update my review if needed as I play through the game. Love it!
It would be nice if the player could create a terrain for the city's and add or subtract area as well.
i guess my city is too big and now it keeps crashing after a few minutes of playing. Or if i add a bus line.
You need to update the game and add more things

I have loved city building games since Sim City 2000. Playing a game like that in VR is amazing. There is loads of content, a lot of maps that are huge! IDK why people are expecting the full PC/Console version on the Quest 3. They did a great job bringing this to VR. Even better I got it on sale for $8! Definitely worth it.

I've played cities skylines, this isn't it, nor does it claim to be I guess.
Major issues:
 One other reviewer that I saw said it crashes, it does, on my first run too.
 Next thing I want to mention, blurry textures. Some things are nice and sharp, looking great, others are a blurry mess, unreadable.
 The map toggle.. it'd be nice if you could rotate it to look like holding a map in front of you, without contorting your neck like an ostrich. Easier to draw roads and pipes on a map. 

Minor issues:
 Somewhere I must have clicked on a seagull, it was 'flying', had a job and it was invisible, I didn't see it anywhere. I also clicked on a ped that was sitting, the seagull thing also popped up, when they stood up I clicked again and got a name. 
 Vehicles clip through the ground a lot and have captcha plates. They probably all have the same plate and it's garbled so it's less noticeable.
 Can't zoom out very far to get a better look.
If you like city builders you will like this! Simple as that

It s really fun but it has not unlocked its full potential MTG games lease add airports airplanes and custom logos. On top of that you should add plot expansions and upd the game as often as possible signed totaly aben
It feels im playing a demo version

This game is amazing and has so much potential. But with that said, you can’t do much. It’s really cut back in the ways of you can’t expand, no airports, larger upgrades nonexistent. It’s like the Xbox game got kneecapped and lost 60% of its content. That’s what it feels like. I severely hope the developers will read this and continue doing updates and refine this game. It is amazing and it has so much potential. I really want to see this game for what it can be.

reviews.review_title: Needs a lot more of everything.
reviews.review_description: Do not compare this to the Xbox game.
This game is so fun And it’s bright. I recommend. It’s kinda hard to get used to, but it’s definitely worth the price￼
If it was a fully featured game it would be great. Too limited, not enough to do

I would avoid buying this game until the devs fix a bug whereby a 2/3 full map starts crashing every 5mins. The game is therefore UNPLAYABLE once you reach a certain point. 
If this is happening to you, I suggest you add a 1-star review, to motivate the dev team to fix what is clearly a bug. 
I've had no response from support[at]fasttravelgames[dot]com, but feel free to email them if you are in the same boat. 
Noting I've tried reinstalling the app and starting a new city - same end result.
May 2026 - It looks like the game is not being updated, and I can see at least one other person who has posted a review about it crashing. I would steer clear until there's an update
More map or map editor!!!!

Yeah the games nice but we need a WAYY bigger map and DLC’s because overtime it’s going to get boring using the same things over and over again. Why can’t we i don’t know give parts of our city names you know, Make theme parks like a fair, set dates or holidays and more realistic things all while having a bigger space like PlayStation have the what enhanced edition ?? Anyone agree with me??

Controls are great. But it's such a small zone and you can not expand. Pretty much just build a single neighborhood in a few hours and thats it.  Highways and large roads are pointless because the area is so small. No airports. Seaports are useless due to size constraints. No trains.. Busses are useless , never see the population to need them. Powerplants are useless due to small size, a couple wind turbines power whole area. Same for water. It looks like Cities Skylines and controls are really good but it is severely reduced in features and size. Fun for an hour or so when you run out of space and things to do..

The best game on Quest VR! Immersive with diverse building designs that evolve on their own Realistic traffic, people with destinations, and a UI that is easy to follow. I would love to see a future project on this theme in an early New York setting with early 1900s architecture and vehicles from the thirties and fourties. A very talented and dedicate group of professionals. Thanks for offering a respite from the daily grind.
i can completely fill two maps in an hour, this game is a waste of time and money
This game is so fun.

Super Addictive, challenging, good replayablility, graphics are nice, could use a update with bit occlusion and lights, it's a bit flats
Also we like more maps and things like airport theme parks etc..DLC's?! 
But Nice good base

So relax to sit and play on cough or in bed! 
Hours of entertainment
Love it! 

If you doubt, try the trail!

reviews.review_title: The best city game I’ve tried ever tried
reviews.review_description:
I’ve tried SO many city builder games on Xbox phone etc..
This has to be the most money I’ve spent on a game like this but it was SO well worth it WELL DONE!🎉
I was not liking this game at all. It had a strong learning curve. But I didn’t give up. I love the game and play it almost every day.

reviews.review_title: Do NOT buy!! This is not a full game!
reviews.review_description: There are no tile expansions! Your city is limited to a single tile and will forever remain small and lack developments! Sea ports have no use due to severely limited space, there are no stadiums, there are no airports! There are no dams. This is not a complete game, it’s more of a demo version you pay $20 for. Do not buy! Only for very young children looking to learn these type of games, but even then when they want more and to expand they wont be able to, and the game will die as they look for other games that will fill what they are looking for. Don’t waste your money, the game needs an overhaul update to be a full game.

I don't like many games att the store but this one is my favorite now. Its really addicted and a really lovely game. Would love more of these kind of games. It even made me think to buy the regular Skyline game but that wouldn't be the same I guess. But still cool.
Anyway Cities VR IS real cool.

I am having troubles with this game and wrote support repeatedly but no help. Someone responded eventually and sent me a video unrelated to the problem. I have been ignored since telling support the video was not the issue I was having and again explaining the problem.
I didnt enjoy it, very poor graphics, complicated game. I wish to refund.

reviews.review_title: Crashes once your city is large
reviews.review_description: Looked promising but the game crashes all the time once your city approaches 20000 as other users noted. The game is alpha at best and they charge you with real money... I would like my money back.

reviews.review_title: I give up. Completely abandoned game
reviews.review_description: This game has so much potential but it had been completely abandoned by the developers. Such a shame.

reviews.review_title: Second update: Please save your money.
reviews.review_description:
This game was incredible. 

My initial review only had the con of a few bugs and build limit. My second update I ran into a significant problem. My main city with a population nearing 20,000 on the pine creek map kept crashing my game. I wrote my second review hoping to get a response from the devs like a few other reviews got, yet I received no response to my concerns from the devs. I gave the game 2 stars and really wanted to find the love for the game again. I took a long break from the game but felt a desire to try making a new city. I was having fun, but noticed something was off. After the last patch when they reduced the price they broke even more. The audio settings were all over the place every time I loaded into the game, some audio was just straight up broken, and the three radio stations that added in a recent update just don’t work anymore. I thought it was maybe a software issue so I uninstalled and reinstalled the game. Fixed nothing and I lost my more recent cities too. Decided to maybe try my old city one more time. Crossed my fingers. And it crashed after about 20 minutes. Again. 

The devs went on record in previous comments and said they had no more plans on major updates to the game. It seems like they are abandoning the game and the price reduction was just to squeeze more out of the game. Please don’t get this game. It is fun. It has so much potential. But it has so many issues that just deflate the experience and it is heartbreaking. Several bugs, small build limit the game can’t even handle, and no further support or communication from the devs. Don’t fall for the trap. Save your money. Don’t support a company that won’t support its community.

reviews.review_title: I like this game but 1 thing!
reviews.review_description:
There’s no landscaping





























⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️🥝👌

reviews.review_title: There is potential, but i cannot enjoy..
reviews.review_description:
Alright, the game has potential, to see the city at street level, and see what a citizen sees with your own eyes, and BE in your town is awesome. I will write some con and pros. Unfortunatly, the cons outweight the pros at this point for me.

PROS:
- See the city with your own eyes as a citizen. You actually are there!
- Familiar looks (Cities Skylines looks)
- massive potential.

CONS
- Very, very low graphics. (not that big of an issue for me but for many)
- Small building space... (At least give us some space.. this is SimCity 2013 type of space...)
- Controls are difficult to get used to.
- lots of missing options. (at time of writing, they still update alot to it!)
- No terraforming. So better build very wonky streets! 

i get it has to be done bc it is a Native Oculus Quest game. (Meaning you dont need a pc to run this!) Maybe make a PCVR version with better Graphics, bigger map etc? 
You use the Cities Skylines as the reference game, so people have very big expectations, and this is not even 1/4 of what cities skylines has to offer!

Again, i understand why, but i mean.... maybe call this Cities VR Lite? and make a full version that u have to run on a PC?

This has so much potential, but at this state, i have to put it away until fixes ave been made, and the game is in a more enjoyable state.

If you dont mind the critics i gave, try it out! If you dont mind small building space and just want to walk arround in your own build city/town? Give it a go!

developer_response.body: Hey AtomicANetwork, thank you for taking the time to leave a review. The building space is limitations of the Quest hardware since our cities can get very full of life. The current record population is 51k. I would check out our biggest map 'Northport' if your looking for some extra building space. We are always open to suggestions on how we can better improve the game, feel free to leave these on our feedback page or check-out existing requests from the community - feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr

reviews.review_description:
A great game for those who love building things. This was a first time for me at a building game and have to say, very impressive. Great concepts and graphics. It took me a good 1 hour to learn the mechanics of both controllers especially the interplay between both R and L controllers.
This game is definitely far from easy and the true challenge is in balancing city building and economics. Expect to spend hours and mild addiction is possible. Patience is the best strategy. Would recommend this game if you have the time to spend and watch out for mild vertigo in the later part of the game when repetitive 360 degree views may be required to view your city and solve problems . A thumbs up for the developers

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to leave a review. We really appreciate hearing insights from our players! If you have any specific requests you can log them in our Feature & Issue Tracker so we can investigate the potential of adding them: https://feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr

reviews.review_description: I've read other reviews and wonder if it is me as few others mention this.  I find moving around to be very clunky and a bit nauseating because I find myself zooming in and out and turning left and right, just to get good line for placing a road.   It's also tiring on the wrist to always be pointing down.  I would love an option to move the whole view from flat on the floor to up on a wall, in increments, like the Demeo play area.  I have Skyline on the Switch and love it, and also have a funny feeling I'll be getting it for PC soon.  I just don't know that I'll be able to play this one.  I'll check in for the updates to see.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a review! We did investigate a world tilt option however this had to be removed before launch as we were not happy with it for several reasons. Further investigation is required for a correctly fitting solution. One of the main things with rotating the world like Demeo does as an example is that it felt very strange since our "playing-field" isn't a flat ground, it is hills, slopes etc. This led to it not feeling good to cross the terrain whilst titled, and even led to some nausea. We need to investigate this further to find a solution which fits correctly to our game rather than forcing out an option which wouldn't actually even help players.  You can add your vote on this post on our feedback tracker to receive updates on this: https://feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr/p/world-tilt (edited)

reviews.review_description:
As I Play simCity so there is something need to be developed :
- must add transportation ( airport,train,ballon) and to make control on it
- make decorations for the building colours,textures, green building…..
- About the services specially electricity ⚡️ if you make it underground will be better because It make difficult to continue build the city like water and sanitary underground.
- Roads, why construction of roads come one way only if you add the choose of 2 way in same time, it will be better .
- Need some aids through game  like to reach 5th level like if need police 👮‍♀️, what shall I do ? Not enough writing only, mean you can help like giving hits where to press to solve the problem, workers , thieves….and so on. 
I hope you can make these things to city vr so it will be more good game 👍🏻👍🏻
Thank you

developer_response.body:
UPDATE 06/07/2022: Hi there YasserS87, we have just released our first free content update adding metro stations, tools for traffic routing, a new street level camera view, left-handed support and much more! It would mean a lot to us if you could test this feature out, and take the time to update your review letting us know if this has improved the experience for you 🙌

Thank you for taking the time to write a review! We will keep improving the game with free content, features and fixes. Our first content update will drop in June (Metros & traffic routing). If you have specific requests like the ones you mention, it would be great if you had a chance to log them in our Feature & Issue Tracker so we can investigate the potential of adding them: https://feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr

If you require assistance with your city, feel free to join our Discord (https://discord.gg/fasttravelgames) or email us over at support@fasttravelgames.com & we'd be more than happy to help!

reviews.review_description: Play a bit of the tutorial and was loving it but I cannot find an option to change to left hand… making aim with the right hand is just impossible and so unplayable…

developer_response.body:
UPDATE 29/06/2022: Hi JMHenry, left handed support is now in Cities: VR. Again, we thank you for your patience waiting for this update & agree this accessibility feature should have been implemented at launch. It would mean a lot to us if you could try this out and take the time to update your review letting us know if this has improved the experience for you 🙌 This update also new content such as metro stations, tools for traffic routing, a new street level camera view and much more!

UPDATE: Hi again JMHenry, left handed support is coming to Cities: VR on June 9. We thank you for your patience waiting for this update & we agree this accessibility feature should have been implemented at launch. We hope you take the time to try Cities: VR again & update your review with your latest thoughts on the game.

Thanks for taking the time to post a review, left hand support is being worked on and will be implemented in a future patch. Sorry for not being able to get it into the game at launch. We would also recommend that you check out our Feature/Issue Tracker where you can see the most requested things from players, and Left Hand support is already present there: https://feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr

reviews.review_description:
Thank you I figured out how to save and like the game. I hope you can create a more realistic version soon.

 How do I save progress I have to start over every time that's disconcerting

developer_response.body: Hey GoldieMay, We love the name Cuties VR and thank you for taking the time to leave a review for us! To save the game: Press the button with 3 lines on the left controller & then click 'save game'.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 5 star review! We really appreciate it! If you have specific requests you can log them in our Feature & Issue Tracker so we can investigate the potential of adding them: https://feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr

reviews.review_description: Great city build game. Soft to slide into and  create your dream city.  Lots of options and a great learning curve.

developer_response.body: Hey Henpar, Thank you for leaving a 5 star review! We hope to see your dream city in our Fast Travel Community Discord https://discord.gg/fasttravelgames

reviews.review_description: Edit: As Cities Vr Enhanced Edition just came out, that is the game we are looking for. So players who played Cities Skyline, you are looking for enhanced edition which is not available on quest 2. For this version The gameplay is amazing, challenging in the beginning, with lots of goals to achieve. Unfortunately, after 2 hours of playing on the same map you reach a point where nothing happens anymore. I currently played 4 hours on a single map and i have build on every inch of land possible, optimized costs and traffic, payed my loans and built every building available.Now I have a budget of 400,000 dollars and nothing i can purchase with them, so nothing anymore happens.The limited space is really killing the experience for me. I really wanted some challenges after a certain point, to be able to link cities in between them or something else. I have no more incentive to play unless i start over again on a new map with no continuation. And also, how do you bring tourists in your city?
Very much like Sim city 5 but easier.

reviews.review_title: My expectations for this was very high!
reviews.review_description:
On the console and the computer, I have put over hundreds of hours. When I bought this, that sticker of 30 bucks. That told me this thing is going to be good or it looks half good. Bummer it was not true, I know this is still 20 maybe 30 days in the release￼, but come on. (They will still update it overtime) The options that can be done are basic. The game is so slow; i’m not talking about performance. Triple speed means quick￼￼, not turtle speed. Income does not match United States; maybe a option menu for that would be great. There is no music options; moreover, Advanced￼ road options like traffic control. How am I going to deal with road traffic. It does not make sense to have a small location and not have the tools to make it more efficient. They’re only buses and boats as public transportation. The concept of the bus lines are way better, than the computer and consoles. The small movements in my arm cause me to miss the location I was aiming for, when I was placing my bus stops. That’s frustrating. There’s a tourist number, ￼but does not really matter. There’s not enough tourist options to buy. Really nothing actually, parks are not tourist locations￼. Graphics are what you expect￼. Education doesn’t really do much instead stopping crime. ￼

Bugs

* The glitching of aiming * The random exits to Passthrough mode * Me being bored

Additions or Wants

* more option for walkway paths ￼* Data for walkway paths * additional data for public transportation * Music options ￼* battery notification timer ￼* research for universities * add more data for trash(proximate data is not that good) * One day, transfer data from console and computer * Advance options for hub(It’s too small￼) * Options for additional ￼hubs that stay on the screen

Needed Changes

* making gameplay faster(could allow me to better improve design structures) * Make universities and high schools￼ cheaper￼ * Late game boredom The developer feedback gets another star￼. Will be keeping the game and looking forward to the following updates.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE 06/07/2022: Hi there GotasdeLuna, we have just released our first free content update adding metro stations, tools for traffic routing, a new street level camera view, left-handed support and much more! It would mean a lot to us if you could test this feature out, and take the time to update your review letting us know if this has improved the experience for you  🙌

Hi there, sad to see that you feel Cities: VR is two star, but we appreciate hearing your insights. Cities: VR is not a port, but an adaptation of the Cities: Skylines experience made specifically for VR. In our next free content update coming this June (Metros & Traffic Routing) you will be able to do many of the things you have mentioned here. Since before launch we have committed to providing free content updates, feature improvements & bug-fixes. We are always open to suggestions for how we can better improve the game (like the amazing ones you have mentioned), feel free to leave these on our feedback page feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr, you can also vote on existing requests.

reviews.review_description: I wish it more like city skylines for example larger maps, district zoning, etc but it is still a good game. EDIT: and this game is worth every penny

Thank you so much for taking the time to leave a review, great to to hear you like it! We will keep adding free content and features to the game.

If you have the time, it would be great if you could log any requests like the ones you mention in our Feature/Issue Tracker here, so we can investigate what potential we have to implement them: https://feedback.fasttravelgames.com/cities-vr

reviews.review_description: The game itself is so much fun. It doesn’t really get boring, the overall visuals of the game are great, and the game’s quality is through the roof. Controls are relatively easy, but the only thing I would say is that the tutorial needs to explain things a little more.

developer_response.body: Great to hear you find it amazing! The tutorial is getting improved in the first upcoming patch which is not far away, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: This is a great game with cute design. But there are bugs. While I was building a 2nd metro station for transport I needed water for it so I connected the pipes from the water but it wouldn’t go through I even made a new water pumping plant and it turns out I couldn’t even put the water through the pipes any more but it’s a good game

developer_response.body: Hi there! Please could you send us a video of this issue to support@fasttravelgames.com so we can take a closer look? :-)

reviews.review_description: I play the original Cities Unlimited for PC and think this Quest 2 port is pretty cool. It makes me feel very much at home with the feel of the graphics. I just wish they would make the play area much bigger because right now it seems just as big as one square on Cities Unlimited.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a review! The highest population in Cities: VR is currently 52, 840, can you challenge yourself to beat it? :-)

reviews.review_title: Auto save not a thing in VR!?!?!
reviews.review_description: Okay so the game is good and fun and all. But it’s lacking vital things that the PC version has. How in the world is there not an auto save option!?!? That’s absolutely trash! The fact I spent 10 hours creating a super dope profitable city waiting to make sure that the population was going correctly had perfectly placed hospitals universities and everything just to come back and find all the progress gone! PC has auto saves as long as you make the initial save game. It’s absolutely ridiculous how this game lacks so many features the PC has. Obviously the oculus is less equip than a PC. But dang this is something so simple that’s left out. This is something that I would literally take to heart and not open the game again over. I just lost 10 hours of progress to have the perfect city that was ready to expand just to lose it all. I now see what these other reviews mean when they say the DEVs clearly left out vital things to the game and just don’t care enough to update it. Shame on the DEVS

developer_response.body: Hi JoshY305, auto-save has now been implemented with the ability to change the autosave frequency. It would mean a lot to us if you could test this feature out, and take the time to update your review letting us know if this has improved the experience for you 🙌.

reviews.review_description:
Really good but could use some more stuff


If you can add mods or view, special maps If you added that came with your five star+++￼
This game is crap.  Not at all like i thought it would be. Waste of money.

developer_response.body: Hello, we're sad to hear that you didn't enjoy the game. We just released a new update with a bunch of improvements and fixes. If you decide to check out the game again, we'd love to hear what you think!
